evals.prompt: Spawn this: refactor AuthService to use a token bucket rate limiter, update the auth docs, and add a smoke test.
evals.expected_output: Plans 3 sibling tasks with disjoint `files` and a `validate` per task. Library + docs + smoke test are independent. Emits the plan as JSON in chat for the disjoint-files check, then asks `proceed? [y / abort]`. On approval, dispatches all 3 in the same assistant turn via parallel Agent tool calls (each with `isolation: "worktree"`, no `cwd`), parses each child's final JSON, creates squad/<id>/integration from BASE, switches and cherry-picks `<base>..<task.branch>` for each `done` task in plan order. After integration, removes each picked task's worktree and source branch. Never pushes. Prints 'Integration branch ready: ... Run /ship:commit to ship.'
Refuses during plan: this is a single-agent task. Tells the user to do it directly. Doesn't dispatch.

evals.prompt: Spawn this: task A reads task B's output to compute its own.
evals.expected_output: Refuses: squad is fan-out, not a pipeline. Tells the user to merge A and B into one task or run them sequentially themselves.

evals.prompt: Spawn this — two of the three tasks both edit src/auth/service.ts.
evals.expected_output: Refuses during plan: two parallel tasks share a writable file. The disjoint-files check is run mechanically against the JSON plan before any Agent dispatch. Tells the user to merge them or pick a different decomposition. Doesn't dispatch.

evals.prompt: Spawn this goal where the cherry-pick of one child's branch conflicts with another's.
evals.expected_output: Runs `git cherry-pick --abort`, prints conflicting paths and the two tasks' titles, stops and asks the user to resolve manually or merge those tasks into one. Doesn't attempt automatic resolution. Leaves the conflicting task's Claude-managed worktree and branch intact for inspection (only successfully picked tasks get cleaned up).

evals.prompt: Spawn this 3-task goal with --yes; all three children commit cleanly.
evals.expected_output: Skips the checkpoint (--yes), dispatches all 3 in the assistant turn that processed the request, parses returns, cherry-picks all onto squad/<id>/integration. After integration succeeds, removes each task's source worktree (`git worktree remove`) and source branch (`git branch -D`) — the cleanup that prevents Claude-managed branches from accumulating across runs. Prints the standard hand-off line.

evals.prompt: Set up squad in this repo.
evals.expected_output: Detects CLAUDE.md (or creates it), appends/replaces the block from skills/setup/block.md between <!-- skrrt:squad --> markers, runs `git check-ignore -q .claude/settings.local.json` and warns if the file isn't ignored, then writes CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 to .claude/settings.local.json (preserving every other key). Reminds the user to reopen Claude Code if their session predates the write.
